scenario_results.name: AWS production compute sizing and capacity architecture
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves markdown compute architecture guidance with EC2 family selection, Auto Scaling group structure, zone placement, quota checks, cost-performance tradeoffs, validation, rollback, source IDs, assumptions, and tenant evidence.

scenario_results.name: Azure VM Scale Sets placement and lifecycle guidance
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves implementation guardrails for Azure VMSS orchestration, zone spread, image lifecycle, health probes, rolling upgrade constraints, monitoring evidence, owner approvals, and rollback.

scenario_results.name: Google Compute Engine managed instance group autoscaling plan
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves a capacity and resiliency decision log for managed instance groups, autoscaling, regional placement, quota, health checks, machine families, update policies, and cost-risk assumptions.

scenario_results.name: Kubernetes node pressure and eviction incident runbook
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves incident runbook fidelity for node conditions, eviction evidence, metrics, autoscaler logs, containment, rollback, and routing when Kubernetes platform or application ownership becomes primary.

scenario_results.name: Backup and recovery verification for stateful VM workload
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves backup, restore, DR, RTO/RPO, owner, and failed restore-test evidence handling without claiming compliance or risk-acceptance authority.

scenario_results.name: Patch and image refresh maintenance runbook
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves routine maintenance runbook output with pre-checks, canary rings, batch sizing, drain or stop/start criteria, telemetry validation, rollback, and application-owner validation handoff.

scenario_results.name: Spot, preemptible, reserved, and on-demand tradeoff
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves cost, reliability, and workload isolation tradeoff handling while refusing purchase, budget, or risk-acceptance approval.

scenario_results.name: Multi-cloud compute baseline portability request
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves refusal to provide one-size-fits-all cloud compute guidance and requires provider-specific EC2, VMSS, managed instance group, policy, and evidence adaptation.

scenario_results.name: Pure application performance tuning refusal
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves explicit refusal and routing for application code, application architecture, SQL tuning, and database schema work.

scenario_results.name: Cloud networking deep design refusal
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves refusal of transit routing, firewall, DNS, carrier, and private connectivity design when not narrowly caused by compute placement.

scenario_results.name: IAM, endpoint, and managed security product routing
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves adjacent specialist routing for IAM, endpoint security, SIEM, key management, and managed security product tuning while preserving compute node hardening context.

scenario_results.name: General help-desk intake refusal
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves refusal of general service management, help-desk intake, non-cloud endpoint operations, and workplace technology work.

scenario_results.name: Missing workload facts low-confidence escalation
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves low-confidence escalation when workload profile, growth, RTO/RPO, utilization, region, zone availability, and budget facts are absent.

scenario_results.name: Compliance or legal decision escalation
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves compliance, contractual, legal, and attestation boundary handling while producing a compute evidence package outline.

scenario_results.name: Conflicting provider guidance and tenant policy
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves source-policy conflict escalation rather than unsupported synthesis when provider guidance, tenant policy, and contractual availability commitments conflict.

scenario_results.name: Tenant-specific adaptation with stricter isolation
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves tenant-specific adaptation for isolation, dedicated host constraints, approved regions, backup vault policy, and change windows while preserving public-source traceability.

scenario_results.name: IaC review for compute lifecycle availability
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves IaC guardrail review for launch templates, VMSS image versions, instance templates, autoscaling minimums, replacement behavior, Terraform state, drift, plan evidence, and rollback.

scenario_results.name: Related request routed to Kubernetes platform specialist
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves routing when Kubernetes upgrade sequencing, CNI, admission policy, namespace isolation, and service mesh design dominate the request.

scenario_results.name: Provider outage and support authority boundary
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves safe incident evidence collection and contingency options while refusing provider root-cause authority and direct failover execution.

scenario_results.name: No-regression baseline preservation check
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves legacy supported tasks, inputs, output shapes, refusal rules, minimum scenario count, and no-regression expectations are preserved or exceeded by the refreshed spec pack.

notes: All 20 refreshed scenarios passed against the standard validation profile and exceed the legacy minimum scenario count of 10.

Scenario coverage includes compute architecture, capacity and resiliency decision logs, incident and maintenance runbooks, implementation guardrails, source refresh, tenant evidence, low-confidence escalation, source-policy conflict handling, tenant adaptation, and explicit adjacent-lane routing.
No unreviewed regressions were recorded; the legacy built package is superseded as baseline context by this spec-first validated package.
